title: WhatsApp introduces usernames to keep phone number private
summary: The messaging platform allows reserving a username and using it instead of the phone number, improving privacy.

title: South Korea announces multi-million dollar investment in memory chips and artificial intelligence
summary: The South Korean government, together with Samsung and SK Hynix, will invest more than 500 billion dollars in new memory factories and humanoid robots.

title: AI programming agents evolve amid new models, tools, and security risks
summary: From open-source models like Ornith-1.0 and Qwen 3.6 to mobile assistants like Cursor, and alerts about vulnerabilities in Claude Code that allow malware execution without verification.

title: Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron sued for alleged DRAM price fixing
summary: The class action lawsuit alleges that the three manufacturers deliberately reduced production of DDR3 and DDR4 to raise prices, taking advantage of the shift to HBM.

title: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 returns to European stores for around 333 euros
summary: Five years after its launch, the 12 GB RTX 3060 is back available in Europe at a similar price to the original, confirming NVIDIA's strategy.

title: Dbrand withdraws its Companion Cube case for Steam Machine after Valve's legal threat
summary: The accessory manufacturer cancelled the Portal 2-based product after Valve requested its removal for copyright infringement, admitting it did not have a license.

title: SteamOS advances with beta 3.8.20 and first PC preinstalled with Valve's system arrives
summary: The update improves VRAM management and graphics, while the Steamroller becomes the first desktop PC to include SteamOS from the factory.

title: Sony hints at a handheld console, promises not to sell hardware at a loss, and crushes Microsoft in GTA VI pre-orders
summary: The Japanese company suggests it is working on a handheld, assures PlayStation will not be sold at a loss, and acknowledges the high cost of memory for future consoles.

title: Apple fixes more than 30 bugs, forecasts record sales, and plans a memory solution with a sanctioned Chinese company
summary: The security patches include bugs in WebKit discovered by AI; in addition, Apple is looking for memory alternatives with a firm on the US blacklist and dates for the iPhone 18 are leaked.

title: Google rations access to Gemini, prepares 50W Qi2 wireless charging, and releases a massive Android security patch
summary: Compute capacity saturation forces limiting Gemini to some clients; fast Qi2 charging is expected in future Pixel and iPhone devices, and 121 vulnerabilities are fixed in Android.

title: AMD ZLUDA CUDA emulator loses funding but adds support for PhysX
summary: The project returns to being a hobby after running out of funds, although it manages to run 32-bit PhysX on AMD GPUs thanks to its version 6.

title: AMD will integrate low-power cores similar to Intel's LPE in future Zen 6
summary: The company confirms that some Zen 6 CPUs will include a new type of efficient core, with support already in preparation for Linux.

title: Imec details its roadmap to 0.3 nm in 2038 with CFET transistors
summary: The Belgian institute redefines Moore's Law and establishes that CFET transistors will be viable at the 0.7 nm node, with cell density as a key indicator.

title: Tencent closes a $2.94 billion deal with Chinese memory manufacturer CXMT
summary: In the midst of the semiconductor trade war, Tencent secures DRAM memory supply with CXMT, while China strengthens its manufacturing capacity.

title: US Supreme Court declares that geofence warrants require constitutional protection
summary: The ruling establishes that geofence warrants violate the Fourth Amendment, a significant advance for digital privacy according to activists.

title: Rocket Lab continues its expansion by acquiring satellite company Iridium
summary: The space company adds Iridium to its portfolio, strengthening its presence in the satellite communications market.

title: ASUS launches the 12.3-inch ROG Strix XG129C touch monitor as a secondary display for 240 €
summary: The device connects via USB-C and serves as an auxiliary touch screen to improve productivity, with a launch price of 239 euros.

title: AMD releases Radeon Adrenalin 26.6.4 drivers while performance issues in Windows 10 persist
summary: The new version of the graphics software is now available, but users report drops in gaming performance on Microsoft's operating system.

title: Anthropic deploys its Claude AI at half price for California, Amazon distills its models, and the EU seeks to attract the company
summary: An agreement with Governor Newsom allows the state government to use Claude at a reduced rate; Amazon optimizes Anthropic's models to lower costs; Austria proposes incentives for Anthropic to set up in Europe.

title: First reviews of Steam Machine highlight its high price and memory performance
summary: Benchmark tests show differences between single and dual memory, while critics agree that the current price makes adoption difficult compared to traditional consoles.
